notes: Local via Ollama. `ollama pull qwen2.5:7b-instruct`. Same model family as the reference qwen-7b-local row, served through Ollama instead of llama.cpp. [independent_value: macro_off]

rawText: The PR titled "fix: null pointer dereference in parser" is authored by dev1 and has not been marked as a draft. You can view it [here](https://github.com/acme/parser/pull/41).
The PR titled "feat: add dark mode toggle" has been fetched. It was created by user @dev2 and adds a theme switch. You can view it [here](https://github.com/acme/ui/pull/42).
The PR titled "docs: clarify the quickstart steps" is authored by dev3 and can be viewed [here](https://github.com/acme/docs-site/pull/43). There are no labels assigned to this pull request.
The PR titled "test: add coverage for the retry path" is authored by dev4 and can be viewed [here](https://github.com/acme/core/pull/44). There are no labels assigned to this pull request.
The PR titled "chore: bump dependencies" is authored by dev5 and can be viewed [here](https://github.com/acme/core/pull/45).

The PR titled "Fix crash when the upload buffer is empty" has been fetched. It was created by user dev6 and is a fix for a regression from PR #40. You can view it [here](https://github.com/acme/uploader/pull/46).
The PR titled "Add support for cursor-based pagination" by user dev7 has been fetched. It implements a new endpoint parameter and you can view it [here](https://github.com/acme/api/pull/47).
The issue titled "App crashes on startup after the latest update" is reported by user reporter1. It contains a stack trace and has no labels or comments yet.
Issue 52 titled "Add support for incoming webhooks" was reported by reporter2. It has one comment and is about adding support for incoming webhooks to help integrations.
The issue titled "Documentation for the auth flow is missing" was reported by user @reporter3. There are no labels or comments on this issue yet.

The issue titled "Login button does nothing when clicked" has been reported by user @reporter4. It mentions that no network request fires when the login button is clicked, and there are 2 comments on this issue.
The previous call's result is already in the transcript. Surface the result to the user, or escalate to the fallback adapter.

The CODEOWNERS file indicates that for changes in the `src/ui/` directory, "bob" should be a reviewer. Given PR 62 tweaks the login form layout, it likely involves UI changes, so we can suggest "bob" as a reviewer.

The CODEOWNERS file indicates that the PR should be reviewed by user dana for changes in the `.github/` directory. However, it doesn't specify other reviewers. You might want to involve more team members depending on the scope of the pull request.
The pull request 64 is titled "Optimize the scheduler loop" and was created by author4. You can view it [here](https://github.com/acme/core/pull/64).
I don't have a tool to check real-time weather. You can check a weather website or app for the current weather in Tokyo.

To create a generic release notes template, we typically include sections like `Version`, `Release Date`, `Highlights`, `New Features`, `Improvements`, `Bug Fixes`, and `Known Issues`. Here’s a simple template you can use:

```
## Version: [Version Number]
## Release Date: [Date]

### Highlights
- Brief overview of the release.

### New Features
- Feature 1 description.
- Feature 2 description.

### Improvements
- Improvement 1 description.
- Improvement 2 description.

### Bug Fixes
- Bug Fix 1 description.
- Bug Fix 2 description.

### Known Issues
- Issue 1 description.
- Issue 2 description.
```

You can fill in the placeholders with specific details as you prepare for each release. If you need to fetch recent issues or pull requests to populate some of these sections, let me know!
